data.title: Paramount Skydance ups breakup fee to $5 billion in bid for Warner Bros. Discovery - Bloomberg
data.description:
• Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has more than doubled the proposed breakup fee in its offer to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to $5 billion, as part of a sweetened proposal designed to outshine rival bids, Bloomberg News reported.
  • The sum would be paid to Warner Bros. if a deal is agreed to but not consummated, the report added.
  • It’s a sign Paramount has confidence its proposed merger would pass muster with regulators, the sources said, who asked not to be identified because the terms haven’t been made public. Paramount had earlier proposed a $2.1 billion breakup fee.
  • Shares of Paramount Skydance (PSKY) and Netflix (NFLX) fell in midday trading on Wednesday as the two media companies emerged as top bidders and are expected to butt heads for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) assets.

data.title: Dollar Tree raises full-year comp outlook to 5–5.5% with multi-price strategy driving Q4 targets
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dollar Tree, Inc. (DLTR) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Michael Creedon, CEO, described Q3 as the start of a "new era for Dollar Tree, one company, one brand, one focus." He reported "a high-quality quarter accompanied by mid-single-digit comps, above outlook earnings and strong end-of-quarter momentum heading into the holidays." He emphasized the first positive year-over-year mix shift in discretionary since Q1 2022 and highlighted "3 million more households" shopping in Q3 compared to last year, with 60% of these from higher-income brackets.
  • Creedon stated "Comparable sales increased 4.2%, a nice acceleration from the quarter-to-date trend of 3.8%." He highlighted the success of the multi-price assortment, calling it "one of the most important strategic shifts in Dollar Tree's modern history and it's working."
  • Creedon noted Halloween sales were "over $200 million, an all-time record," driven by multi-price penetration, which generated "3.5x more profit than each non-multi-price item we sold."
  • Stewart Glendinning, CFO, said "Q3 net sales increased 9.4% to $4.7 billion."
  • Glendinning added, "Q3 gross margin expanded 40 basis points to 35.8%." He attributed the EPS improvement to "freight, higher discretionary sales mix and SG&A."

Outlook

  • Glendinning provided Q4 guidance of "comps will come in between 4% and 6%, which should support net sales of $5.4 billion to $5.5 billion and adjusted EPS in the range of $2.40 to $2.60."
  • Full-year guidance was raised to "comp outlook to between 5% and 5.5% and our adjusted EPS outlook to $5.60 to $5.80."
  • Key assumptions include "net sales of approximately $19.35 billion to $19.45 billion, gross margin expansion of approximately 50 to 60 basis points" and "Dollar Tree segment SG&A deleverage of approximately 120 basis points."
  • Glendinning reiterated, "as is customary, we intend to give a detailed outlook for 2026 on our next earnings call in March."

Financial Results

  • Q3 net sales increased 9.4% to $4.7 billion, with comparable sales up 4.2%.
  • Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $1.21.
  • Gross margin expanded 40 basis points to 35.8%, attributed to "strong operational execution and cost discipline."
  • Discretionary comp increased 4.8%, consumables 3.5%.
  • Inventory was down $143 million or 5% versus prior year.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter was negative $57 million, year-to-date generated $88 million.
  • The company repurchased 4.1 million shares for $399 million in Q3, with year-to-date repurchases totaling $1.5 billion.

Q&A

  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about drivers of same-store sales acceleration and comp trends supporting Q4 guidance. Creedon responded, "Halloween was just a great finish to the quarter... our setup for Thanksgiving and Christmas is just fantastic."
  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Inquired about the decline in traffic and its relation to price increases. Creedon explained, "We saw the traffic decelerate in that August, September time frame. That was the peak of our restickering. Those red stickers... was the peak distraction for us."
  • John Heinbockel, Guggenheim: Questioned the divergence between traffic and units. Creedon stated, "When we take multi-price, we'll take away sections of $1.25 and so your units will naturally decline... but we believe the proof point we have from break the dollar was that you took up the price of the whole store."
  • Edward Kelly, Wells Fargo: Asked about multi-price mix next year. Creedon replied, "85% of the store is still $2 or less. So we're still early in this multi-price game."
  • Paul Lejuez, Citi: Asked about Thanksgiving weekend trends and the share of sales above $2. Creedon clarified "85% of the stores $2 or less that is on sales dollars."
  • Rupesh Parikh, Oppenheimer: Asked about elasticity and tariff relief. Creedon said, "the elasticity has really -- it's come in as we've modeled it. It's very manageable."
  • Multiple analysts focused on traffic vs. ticket mix, impact of restickering, and shrink.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a pressing and cautious tone, focusing on traffic declines, elasticity, and risk of price increases. Questions repeatedly addressed the underlying causes of negative traffic and sustainability of margin expansion.
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing execution and strategic shifts, but shifted to a measured and explanatory stance when addressing analyst concerns, especially on traffic and restickering. Creedon frequently referenced prior success and data-driven confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence in the multi-price initiative and guidance was more pronounced, while analysts were more focused on potential risks tied to customer response and cost pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 emphasized balanced growth between traffic and ticket, with positive unit growth and discretionary comps. Q3 showed comps driven entirely by ticket, with slightly negative traffic attributed to restickering and internal disruptions.
  • Guidance was raised in Q3, both for comps and EPS, compared to Q2’s already improved outlook.
  • The multi-price strategy was central in Q3, with explicit profit and margin benefits highlighted; while Q2 focused on rollout and early customer response.
  • Analysts in Q3 were more focused on risk factors of traffic declines and cost controls than in Q2, where the tone was more positive about customer acquisition and share gains.
  • Management described a more unified brand and culture post-Family Dollar sale in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited shrink as higher than last year, though in line with expectations, and outlined ongoing investment in asset protection and technology.
  • Analyst concerns centered on negative traffic, customer pushback on pricing, elasticity, and the impact of restickering.
  • Management indicated that restickering-related costs and disruptions are "basically done" and not expected to repeat next year.
  • SG&A pressures from payroll and restickering were addressed, with expectations for moderation in wage growth and elimination of restickering costs in 2026.

Final Takeaway

Dollar Tree leadership described Q3 as a strong validation of its focused strategy, with above-market comps, margin expansion, and clear benefits from the multi-price initiative. Management emphasized that the company is well positioned for the holiday season and confident in meeting its raised full-year guidance, highlighting operational improvements, strategic discipline, and a broadened customer base as core drivers of continued performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Cal-Maine strengthens Prepared Foods business, launches $15M capacity upgrade
data.description:
Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) is strengthening its prepared foods platform through new leadership appointments and targeted prepared foods production capacity expansions, it said on Wednesday.

Johnathan Zoeller has joined Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) as chief financial officer, Prepared Foods, adding valuable external experience with over 25 years as a senior financial and corporate accounting executive. He most recently served as vice president, treasurer, and head of investor relations for Westlake (WLK).

In parallel, Cal-Maine Foods is launching a new $15 million network optimization and capacity-expansion project at Echo Lake Foods, expected to add 17 million pounds of annual scrambled egg production by mid-fiscal 2027.

The project consolidates all scrambled egg manufacturing into a single, modernized facility, and expands capacity with a new production line at the facility to support both near-term demand and long-term organic growth. 

In total, these planned investments are expected to grow Cal-Maine’s prepared foods production capacity by more than 30% in the next 18-24 months.

data.title: First Majestic prices $300M offering of convertible senior notes
data.description:
• First Majestic Silver (NYSE:AG)  announced on Thursday a $300M offering of unsecured convertible senior notes due 2031.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to repurchase a portion of its outstanding 0.375% convertible senior notes due 2027 and for general corporate purposes, including strategic opportunities.
  • The notes will bear cash interest semi-annually at 0.125% per annum. 
  • The initial conversion rate for the notes will be 44.7227 common shares of the company per $1,000 principal amount of notes, equivalent to approximately $22.36 per share.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 8, 2025.
  • Shares -1.38%.

data.title: Regentis Biomaterials announces pricing of initial public offering for $10M
data.description:
• Regentis Biomaterials (NYSE:RGNT)  announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 1.25M ordinary shares at an initial public offering price of $8.00 per share, for gross proceeds of $10M.
  •  In addition, the company has granted the underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 0.18M ordinary shares to cover over-allotments.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on the NYSE American LLC on December 4, 2025, under the symbol “RGNT.” The offering is expected to close on or about December 5, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds for development activities and preparation of the PMA submission for GerlinC and also for corporate purposes, including the repayment of certain debt.
  • ThinkEquity is acting as sole book-running manager for the offering.

data.title: Axogen gets FDA OK for Avance’s biologics license application
data.description:
• Axogen (AXGN) on Wednesday said that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved the biologics license application for AVANCE (acellular nerve allograft-arwx).
  • Avance is an acellular nerve scaffold for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients aged 1 month or older with sensory, mixed, and motor peripheral nerve discontinuities.
  • Continued approval for these indications depends on verification and description of clinical benefit in confirmatory studies, Axogen (AXGN) added.
  • Commercial availability of the licensed Avance product is expected early in the second quarter of 2026. In the meantime, Avance remains available under the current tissue framework.

data.title: Snowflake in charts: Product revenue jumps 28.7% Y/Y in FQ3, total customers rise
Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
07:30 AM Challenger Job-Cut Report 
 
This monthly report counts and categorizes announcements of corporate layoffs based on mass layoff data from state departments of labor. The job-cut report is basically a rehash of the weekly jobless claims report but provides additional insight into where layoffs are occurring.
 
08:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services
 
Changes in the level of imports and exports, along with the difference between the two, are a valuable gauge of economic trends here and abroad. While these trade figures can directly impact all financial markets, they primarily affect the value of the dollar in the foreign exchange market.
 
08:30 AM Jobless Claims 
 
New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time. An increasing trend suggests a deteriorating labor market. 
 
Claims are seen at 225K, from a low of 216K last week, back toward the 4-week moving average at 223.75K. 
 
10:00 AM Factory Orders 
 
Factory orders represent the dollar level of new orders for both durable and nondurable goods. This report gives more complete information than the advance durable goods report, which is released one or two weeks earlier in the month.
 
Orders expected up 0.2 percent on the month.
 
10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report 
 
The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.
 
12:00 PM Fed's Bowman Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will speak virtually on “Bank Supervision and Regulation” before the Florida Bankers Association Leadership Luncheon.
 
04:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet 
 
The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: NCino raises FY26 revenue and operating income outlook with 11% subscription growth target, driven by AI momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: nCino (NCNO) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Sean Desmond highlighted nCino's increasing momentum in sales and product development, attributing this to strong customer demand and the company's expanding AI-powered platform. Desmond stated, "Sales and product development both picked up momentum in Q3, and I'm very pleased with the level of demand we are seeing from our customers and prospects across market segments, geographies and products."
  • Desmond pointed to AI as a key differentiator, reporting that "over 110 customers" have now purchased Banking Advisor intelligence units and emphasizing the company's development of role-based AI agents and operational analytics. Desmond noted, "We are advancing AI capabilities at a pace well ahead of customers’ ability to adopt them." He also reported early renewals from customers wanting immediate access to AI features.
  • The company reported significant customer outcomes, including a $5 billion U.S. bank eliminating 86% of duplicate data entry and a $5.2 billion institution reducing underwriting from 23 days to 2 days. Desmond shared results from a recent nCino Research Institute analysis, stating, "nCino’s customers exhibit on average a 64% better return on average assets and a 75% superior return on average equity relative to their non-nCino peers."
  • Desmond detailed notable sales wins in the U.S. community and enterprise markets, as well as new and expanded contracts in Japan and EMEA, highlighting the Integration Gateway API solution as an ACV growth driver.
  • CFO Gregory D. Orenstein stated, "Total revenues in the third quarter were $152.2 million, up 10% year-over-year. Subscription revenues were $133.4 million, up 11% year-over-year on a reported basis and 7% organically."
  • Orenstein attributed overperformance to successful execution, mortgage business strength, and favorable FX, while also noting a contract buyout. He added, "Non-GAAP operating income was $39.9 million or 26% of total revenues, representing 600 basis points of operating margin expansion, both year-over-year and quarter-over-quarter."

Outlook

  • Orenstein guided for Q4 total revenues of $146.75 million to $148.25 million and subscription revenues of $130.75 million to $132.25 million, noting a 4% and 5% year-over-year increase at the midpoint. He indicated a 3% organic headwind due to prior one-time revenues.
  • For FY26, the company now targets total revenues of $591.9 million to $593.4 million, up from prior guidance of $585 million to $589 million, and subscription revenues of $520.5 million to $522 million, up from $513.5 million to $517.5 million, representing 11% growth at the midpoint. Non-GAAP operating income is now expected to be $127.2 million to $128.2 million, a 33% increase over the previous year at the midpoint.
  • U.S. mortgage subscription revenues growth guidance for FY26 was raised to approximately 7%, up from 5% previously.
  • The company reiterated its outlook for ACV of $564 million to $567 million for FY26.

Financial Results

  • Subscription revenues reached $133.4 million, and total revenues were $152.2 million for Q3. The company reported professional services revenues of $18.8 million.
  • Non-U.S. total revenues were $33.6 million, with non-U.S. subscription revenues at $27.9 million.
  • nCino ended the quarter with $87.9 million in cash and $203.5 million outstanding on its line of credit.
  • The company completed its $100 million share buyback program, repurchasing approximately 4 million shares at an average price of $25.02 per share.
  • Platform pricing adoption advanced to 27% of ACV, up from 21% last quarter.

Q&A

  • Michael Infante, Morgan Stanley, asked about business leverage and AI efficiencies. Orenstein responded that "we continue to see opportunities in the business for further efficiency. And yes, we're seeing it from AI as well as... healthy cost discipline."
  • Saket Kalia, Barclays, inquired about ACV growth drivers. Desmond described strong execution and customer demand for tech investment and efficiency, noting, "banks remain very aggressive on their tech investment with AI driving the narrative."
  • Alexander Sklar, Raymond James, sought clarification on commercial loan expansion and M&A impacts. Orenstein explained, "about 95% where we were the go-forward platform" in bank M&A events.
  • Charles Nabhan, Stephens, asked about mortgage business outperformance. Desmond cited expansion in top 100 banks and ongoing traction in the IMB space.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive but probing tone, focusing on the sustainability of growth, margin expansion, and the impact of AI and pricing transitions. Questions were constructive, seeking detail on execution and adoption rates.
  • Management’s tone was confident in both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing execution, AI momentum, and platform adoption. Orenstein used phrases such as "we feel good about the business right now" and "we are confident that we are on track."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts continued to display confidence, with slightly more focus on the tangible results of AI strategies and operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • nCino raised full-year revenue and subscription guidance compared to the prior quarter, reflecting higher-than-expected execution and mortgage segment performance.
  • Platform pricing conversion increased from 21% to 27% of ACV.
  • Management’s tone remained confident but focused more on AI adoption and early renewals, with more explicit discussion of the halo effect from AI features. Analysts shifted questions toward the pace of AI-driven adoption and the impact of early renewals.
  • International revenue growth decelerated slightly on an organic basis, while AI and operational analytics initiatives advanced as core themes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged seasonality in revenues, a 3% organic headwind from prior one-time revenues, and the need for continued execution to close Q4 pipeline opportunities.
  • Orenstein noted the non-repeatable nature of certain Q3 revenue sources, such as contract buyouts and FX benefits.
  • Analyst questions noted the need for continued cost discipline, customer adoption of new pricing, and the pace of international growth reacceleration.

Final Takeaway

nCino’s third quarter results demonstrated expanding momentum in both sales and AI-powered product adoption, leading management to raise full-year guidance for revenue, subscription growth, and operating income. The company highlighted the increasing impact of AI features on customer outcomes and early renewals, while reiterating confidence in its execution and positioning for continued growth into fiscal 2027.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: UL Solutions announces pricing of secondary public offering
data.description:
• UL Solutions (NYSE: ULS) announced on Thursday the pricing of its underwritten public offering of 12.5M shares of its Class A common stock at a public offering price of $78.00 per share.
  • The offering consists entirely of secondary shares to be sold by UL Standards & Engagement.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.87M shares of Class A common stock at the public offering price.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 5, 2025.
  • Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and J.P. Morgan (in alphabetical order) are acting as lead managing bookrunners, with BofA Securities acting as a managing bookrunner.
